generated_oml:
instance PositionSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Sensor that measures the horizontal position of the crane trolley along the rail."
    DTDFVocab:producedData CraneStateDataTransmitted
]

instance VelocitySensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Sensor that measures the velocity of the trolley movement."
    DTDFVocab:producedData CraneStateDataTransmitted
]

instance LoadSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Sensor that measures the weight of the load attached to the crane hoist."
    DTDFVocab:producedData CraneStateDataTransmitted
]

instance CraneStateDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom PositionSensor, VelocitySensor, LoadSensor
]

instance CraneStateTimeSeriesData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Timeseries data of crane state stored in the local TimescaleDB, containing rows of timestamped position, velocity, and load information."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo TimescaleDataLogger
    DTDFVocab:fromData CraneStateDataTransmitted
]

instance CraneKinematicModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Kinematic model derived from CAD geometry of the gantry frame, rails, hoist assembly, and trolley, used for trajectory prediction and validation."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo CADModelEnabler
]

instance TimescaleDataLogger : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Enabler that writes physical crane state data to a local TimescaleDB via TCP/IP over the local network."
    DTDFVocab:enables MonitoringService, DataLoggingService
]

instance CADModelEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Enabler that processes CAD geometry of the gantry system into the CraneKinematicModel."
    DTDFVocab:enables SimulationService, ValidationService
]

instance SimulationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service that performs simulation of the CraneKinematicModel for validation and analysis."
]

instance MonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service that streams live crane state data to operator dashboards in real time."
    DTDFVocab:provides LiveCraneStateInsight, HistoricalDataInsight
]

instance DataLoggingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service that manages continuous storing and archival of crane sensor data in TimescaleDB."
    DTDFVocab:provides DataArchiveInsight
]

instance ControlService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service that computes optimal feed‑forward control commands based on desired trajectories and current sensor state."
    DTDFVocab:provides ControlCommandInsight
]

instance ValidationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service that compares predictions from CraneKinematicModel with actual sensor data to assess model fidelity."
    DTDFVocab:provides ModelValidationInsight
]

instance VisualizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service that renders 3D real‑time visualization of the crane geometry overlaid with live sensor data."
    DTDFVocab:provides RealTimeVisualizationInsight
]

instance LiveCraneStateInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real‑time presentation of current position, velocity, and load of the crane."
]

instance HistoricalDataInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Access to historical records of crane state for post‑hoc analysis."
]

instance ControlCommandInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Projected feed‑forward control commands to bring the crane along desired trajectory."
]

instance ModelValidationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Quantitative comparison of simulated versus measured motion trajectories for assessing model fidelity."
]

instance RealTimeVisualizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real‑time 3D visualization of the crane’s kinematic model overlayed with live sensor data."
]

instance DataArchiveInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Information on data storage and retention status in TimescaleDB."
]

instance ControlCommandActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Actuator that sends feed‑forward control requests to the physical crane controller."
]

instance GantryFrame : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Mechanical gantry frame that provides the primary structural support."
]

instance Rails : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Linear rails that guide the horizontal trolley movement."
]

instance HoistAssembly : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Assembly that includes the hoist mechanism for vertical load movement."
]

instance Trolley : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Translational carriage that moves along the rails carrying the load."
]

instance LoadAttachment : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Attachment point for the load being lifted by the crane."
]

instance Controller : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Embedded controller that issues movement commands and receives sensor feedback."
]

instance CraneTwinEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains GantryFrame, Rails, HoistAssembly, Trolley, LoadAttachment, Controller, PositionSensor, VelocitySensor, LoadSensor, ControlCommandActuator
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "Control commands are issued by the Control Application, which uses the Physical Crane Interface to send movement or trajectory instructions to the crane hardware via a Python program. The command format, authentication, and feedback mechanisms are not detailed; execution confirmation is inferred indirectly through the state logs captured by the Logging Application."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The GreenhouseDT explorer orchestrates its digital twin through a micro‑service style architecture that relies heavily on containerization or virtualization for rapid deployment. Sensor data streams from a Raspberry‑Pi based physical greenhouse are published to an Apache ActiveMQ message broker, which decouples the ingestion layer from downstream analytics and control services. The twin’s state is persisted in an InfluxDB time‑series database, accessible via its HTTP API or Telegraf agents. Virtual machine images bundle prerecorded data for replay and testing scenarios, while the entire stack (including the message broker, database, and twin logic) can be assembled from Docker containers for reproducibility. For the Air Quality Management exemplar, the twin leverages an AutomationML model to describe asset relationships and uses Microsoft Azure services (e.g., Azure IoT Hub, Azure Functions, Azure Sentinel) to implement runtime visualization, simulation, and prediction pipelines. Overall, the system coordinates resources via message‑driven event buses, uses cloud or on‑premise deployment patterns, and manages compute and storage through VM image snapshots and Docker images."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "Components communicate via an MQTT broker that facilitates signaling between services such as the Trajectory Generator, Simulation Service, and Continuous Validation Service. The architecture follows a container-based model; specific bandwidth, latency, network topology, or security protocol details are not provided."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "All components run on local infrastructure—a lab environment—using Python applications, a TimescaleDB database, and a Grafana dashboard. Deployment is on‑premise; scalability, cloud support, redundancy, and availability mechanisms are not described."
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "Integration with external systems is achieved through standardized middleware and cloud connectors. Physical subsystems (e.g., Raspberry‑Pi controllers) push telemetry to the twin via ActiveMQ JMS queues, while the twin exposes its state through InfluxDB’s RESTful line‑protocol endpoint for downstream analytics. The Air Quality twin utilizes AutomationML to exchange asset metadata and configuration with enterprise PLM systems or other twins. Azure services—such as IoT Hub for device connectivity, Azure Functions for serverless orchestration, and Azure Sentinel for security analytics—provide managed integration points that connect the twin to broader enterprise IT fabrics. All components are exposed over standard protocols (HTTP, WebSocket for IoT Hub, JMS for ActiveMQ) ensuring interoperability with legacy or third‑party systems."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin model conforms to the AutomationML specification, ensuring machine‑equipment data interoperability with CAPP and CAM systems. Communication between physical and virtual layers uses MQTT, an industry‑wide open‑standard messaging protocol supported by ActiveMQ. Data persistence employs InfluxDB, an open‑source time‑series database that follows the OpenTSDB schema. Version control and collaboration are handled via Git on GitHub, following best practices for open‑source software development."
]
